topic.what: Systematic robustness evaluation of ALEX under static data poisoning and dynamic algorithmic complexity attacks (ACA)
topic.how: Large-scale empirical benchmarks across 4 SOSD datasets with up to 200K adversarial inserts, measuring lookup performance degradation and comparing black-box vs white-box attack variants
topic.why_matters: Learned indexes rely on data distribution models creating exploitable vulnerabilities; prior evaluations are limited in scale or consider only one threat model, leaving robustness under interactive workloads poorly understood

characteristics.what_it_measures: Performance degradation from adversarial keys inserted before index build
characteristics.evidence: Across all datasets (Books, Facebook, Lognormal, Wiki TS) and poisoning levels (5-20% budget), worst-case slowdown below 1.03x
characteristics.context: Static poisoning is insufficient to produce meaningful lookup slowdown; variation dominated by noise

characteristics.what_it_measures: Peak performance degradation from adversarial insertions during runtime
characteristics.vs_baseline: clean index baseline measured after each insertion batch
characteristics.evidence: Lognormal and Wiki TS show strongest effects with 2.7x-2.8x peak; degradation stabilizes at elevated plateau
characteristics.context: Dynamic ACA induces substantially greater and more persistent degradation than static poisoning

characteristics.what_it_measures: Variation in attack impact across different data distributions
characteristics.value: Lognormal/Wiki TS high degradation; Facebook weak degradation
characteristics.evidence: Facebook shows weakest degradation due to narrow clean-rank bin coverage despite appearing spread in value space; lognormal and Wiki TS insertions occupy large fraction of clean-rank bins
characteristics.context: Fraction of clean-rank bins occupied by inserted keys predicts global impact better than value-space distribution

characteristics.what_it_measures: Performance difference between structural knowledge and random insertion strategies
characteristics.value: White-box slightly higher at peak; black-box matches/exceeds at end-state
characteristics.evidence: Peak degradation slightly higher for white-box but inconsistent across datasets; at end-state black-box often matches or exceeds white-box degradation
characteristics.context: Structural knowledge provides only marginal advantage; randomized insertion strategies are often sufficient to induce substantial slowdown

characteristics.what_it_measures: Need for clean key baselines to isolate adversarial effects from distribution sensitivity
characteristics.value: Required for distinguishing attack signal from inherent distribution sensitivity
characteristics.evidence: Naturally skewed key distributions can produce similar narrow clean-rank bin coverage resembling adversarial conditions
characteristics.context: Real-key control baseline essential for rigorous robustness evaluation

characteristics.value: up to 200K adversarial inserts; dynamic experiments use up to 10^6 keys with subsampling
characteristics.evidence: Scaling to realistic workloads with up to 200K adversarial insertions across multiple SOSD datasets
characteristics.context: Larger scale than prior evaluations which used smaller or synthetic datasets

good_for: Evaluating robustness of learned indexes against adversarial insertions
Understanding interaction between threat model and data distribution
Comparing static vs dynamic attack effectiveness
Dataset sensitivity analysis for learned index security
not_for: Generalization to other learned index designs beyond ALEX
requires: Write access to index for data poisoning
Multiple real-world datasets with diverse key distributions
Control workloads with clean keys for isolation

novelty: Unified framework comparing static poisoning vs dynamic ACA on learned indexes
Large-scale evaluation with up to 200K adversarial insertions
First demonstration that dynamic ACA causes substantially more degradation than static poisoning
Evidence that black-box attacks match white-box effectiveness at end-state
Clean-rank bin fraction as predictor of attack impact over value-space distribution
